pr.title: Match 8 functions across stage, fighter, and menu TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Matched eight previously unmatched or partially unmatched functions across stage, fighter, and game/menu translation units, with one header signature refinement. The PR replaced placeholders and adjusted existing code in Brinstar, Big Blue route, Poké Floats, Kirby hat specials, Popo specials, fighter dynamics, knockback parameter code, and gm_1601 selection-history code. The body reports clean `ninja main.dol` linking and 100.0% objdiff matches for the eight targeted functions. A prior attempt also included three Ice Mountain matches, but that commit was dropped after CI exposed a 4-byte regression from a const-section reorder.
what_changed: Implemented or matched `grZebes_801D8558` stage gobj setup logic and filled Brinstar static stage data/callback table entries including `grZe_callbacks`, `grZe_803E1A10`, `grZe_803E1B20`, and `grZe_803E1B2C`.

Implemented or matched Big Blue route data and functions in `grbigblueroute.c`, including `grBb_Route_803E5E78`, the stage descriptor-style object `grBb_Route_803E617C`, file/string data, an adjusted `OSReport` call using stored format/file strings, and `grBigBlueRoute_8020C530` random available-route index selection.

Matched `grPura_80211FD8` by correcting the helper signature/call for `grPura_80213030` to take `Ground_GObj* arg0`, then preserving the teardown sequence through `grPura_80212EF4`, `Ground_801C2FE0`, `grPura_80213030`, `mpLib_80055E24(0x18)`, and `lb_800115F4`.

Implemented `ftCo_8009CB40` in `ftdynamics.c` and refined `ftCo_8009DB50` for Kirby-as-Jigglypuff hat dynamics setup, including dynamic bone count assertion, per-bone flag updates, `lb_8000FD48`, `FtPart_TopN`, and `lb_80011710` usage.

Matched `ftCo_800D0EC8` by swapping the first and third arguments to `ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback`, returning the negated result of `ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback(p_ftCommonData->x310, fp->x34_scale.y, Fighter_804D6524->x30)`.

Adjusted Kirby Peach-hat air end logic in `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90`: added extern float constants `ftKb_Init_804D9570` and `ftKb_Init_804D9574`, selected motion states via `cmd_vars[0]` and hat kind, and used those constants as motion-state rate/blend parameters instead of literal `1.0F` and `0.0F`.

Matched Popo Special-Hi air-start animation behavior in `ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim`, adding stack padding, using `ftPp_SpecialHi_801220D4` for the out-of-range fallback and `ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DD8` after startup frames end, and tightening `checkNanaInRange` through a `Vec3*` access pattern and explicit `ftNn_Init_8012300C(nana_gobj) == 1` check.

Expanded `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll` logic in the same Popo file to inline the transition previously abstracted by `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll_Land`, including `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, callback restoration, accessory callback setup, and air drift clamp.

Implemented `gm_8016A9E8` as a two-array MRU push routine over `lbl_8046B668`, scanning for the first `arr2[i] == -2` sentinel, shifting existing entries, writing `(arg0,arg1)` to slot zero, and returning the new count/index value.
Updated `gm_1601.h` from `UNK_RET gm_8016A9E8(UNK_PARAMS)` to `int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1)` to support the matched implementation.
smart_moves: Kept the PR focused on matches that linked cleanly and reached 100.0% objdiff, while explicitly dropping a separate Ice Mountain const-section reorder that CI showed caused a 4-byte regression.

Used exact global/static data layout reconstruction for stage files rather than only matching code bodies; `grZebes` and `grbigblueroute` both required callback tables, descriptor structs, file strings, flags, and report strings to line up.
Used stored extern float constants in `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90` instead of equivalent literals, a common decomp tactic when data references are required for instruction/data relocation matching.

Adjusted expression shapes and access paths for matching, such as direct `gobj->user_data` casts in Kirby, a `Vec3*` temporary for Nana position, explicit boolean/integer comparisons, and `PAD_STACK` calls.
Refined a header prototype only after the implementation established the needed typed signature, reducing `UNK_PARAMS` while preserving evidence from the matched body.

For the Big Blue route `OSReport`, reused the reconstructed descriptor's format string and file string rather than relying on `__FILE__`, indicating attention to string/data references that affect matching.
decomp_lessons: For stage setup functions, matching may require reconstructing the surrounding stage descriptor and `StageCallbacks` table, not just the target function body.
Do not reorder const/static data casually to make one function match; neighboring functions and section layout can regress even when the target function improves.
Header cleanup should be driven by known matched signatures. Here `gm_8016A9E8` became `int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1)` after the body showed the argument and return types.
Equivalent-looking literals may not match when the original code references named data; use extern globals such as `ftKb_Init_804D9570` and `ftKb_Init_804D9574` when relocation behavior demands it.
Small source-shape changes matter for matching: explicit `== 1`, temporary pointer choice, repeated `gobj->user_data` casts, and stack padding all appeared in the diff as match tactics.

For array-history or MRU routines, preserving sentinel semantics and exact loop bounds is important; `gm_8016A9E8` scans 27 entries for `-2`, shifts both arrays together, and writes the newest pair at index 0.
When a helper prototype is wrong, matching a caller can expose the real parameter list, as with `grPura_80213030(Ground_GObj* arg0)`.
naming_conventions: Stage callbacks use per-stage prefixes and address-style suffixes, e.g. `grZebes_801D8558`, `grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4`, `grPura_80211FD8`.
Stage static data names retain project address-style labels and stage prefixes, e.g. `grZe_803E1A10`, `grZe_803E1B20`, `grZe_803E1B2C`, `grBb_Route_803E5E78`, `grBb_Route_803E617C`.
Fighter code follows character/module prefixes such as `ftKb_` for Kirby, `ftPp_` for Popo, `ftCo_` for common fighter code, and `ftNn_` for Nana.
Callback table type names such as `StageCallbacks` are preserved, with fields filled by function pointers or `NULL` entries rather than invented semantic names.
Unknown or partially understood structure fields remain address/offset-like or placeholder-style, e.g. `x2222_b2`, `x2219_b0`, `unk528`, `unk52c`, `unkCCB8`, avoiding overnaming without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(32)`, or `PAD_STACK(8)` in fighter functions to match stack frame shape.
Changed source expression order in `ftCo_800D0EC8` so `ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback` receives `p_ftCommonData->x310` first and `Fighter_804D6524->x30` third.
Used explicit casts from `gobj->user_data` instead of the local `fp` in parts of `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90`, likely to reproduce load/register behavior.
Replaced float literals in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` with extern global floats `ftKb_Init_804D9574` and `ftKb_Init_804D9570`.
Used `__assert(__FILE__, ...)` in dynamics code and reconstructed stored format/file strings in Big Blue route reporting to match file/string reference behavior.
Expanded an inline-like helper path in `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll` rather than calling the previous helper, improving match shape for the function.
Reconstructed static arrays and structs in stage files so callback/data references resolve exactly.
Dropped a commit involving `gricemt.c` const-section reorder after CI showed a 4-byte regression in neighboring `grIceMt_801F7A2C`.
review_feedback: No inline review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided slice.

The PR body notes CI caught a 4-byte regression in `grIceMt_801F7A2C` from an earlier `gricemt.c` const-section reorder, leading the author to remove that commit and plan a follow-up PR. This is the only concrete feedback/process signal in the slice.
searchable_terms: Match 8 functions across stage fighter menu TUs
follow_up_queries: Find the follow-up PR that resubmitted the dropped `gricemt.c` matches `fn_801F9338`, `fn_801F9448`, and `fn_801F9558` without the const-section regression.
Search for other PRs that reconstruct `StageCallbacks` tables and stage descriptor structs to match stage setup functions.
Search for additional uses of extern float constants like `ftKb_Init_804D9570` and `ftKb_Init_804D9574` in Kirby motion-state matching.
Look for later refinements of `grBigBlueRoute_8020C530`, especially if the `__assert(__FILE__, 0x2E5, "0")` path or return behavior was revisited.
Check whether `ftCo_8009CB40` later received clearer struct field names for `DynamicsData` or dynamic bone handling.
